# v1.0.0 — Vector RAG vs RDF GraphRAG: the deterministic comparison

First public release of **biomedical-rag-bench**. It ships Project 1: a falsifiable
comparison of five retrieval conditions over the *same* biomedical knowledge (Hetionet +
PubMed), under a shared, additive-only evaluation contract. Every condition is scored on the
same 52 deterministic questions (9 of the 10 question types) with a fixed generator
(`claude-haiku-4-5`, temperature 0) and deterministic judges — so differences reflect
*representation*, not measurement. The fuzzy/semantic class (type 10 → H4) is **deferred to
`v1.1.0`** pending judge calibration; it is not part of this release.

## Headline — deterministic accuracy (pass @ n=52)

| `closed_book` | `vector` | `graph_1hop` | `graph_2hop` | `graph_sparqlgen` |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 3 | 9 | 13 | 13 | **16** |

All five conditions ran clean (0 errored questions) on corpus `full-2c102cb0`.

### Per-type breakdown

| Type | closed | vector | 1hop | 2hop | sparqlgen |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 01 0-hop attribute      | 2/3 | 2/3 | 3/3 | 2/3 | 3/3 |
| 02 1-hop factoid        | 0/5 | 0/5 | **5/5** | 1/5 | 0/5 |
| 03 2-hop traversal      | 0/7 | 0/7 | 0/7 | 1/7 | 2/7 |
| 04 3+-hop traversal     | 0/8 | 0/8 | 0/8 | 0/8 | 0/8 |
| 05 aggregative          | 0/8 | 0/8 | 0/8 | 0/8 | **8/8** |
| 06 set intersection     | 0/5 | 0/5 | 0/5 | 0/5 | 0/5 |
| 07 set difference       | 0/5 | 0/5 | 0/5 | 0/5 | 0/5 |
| 08 negative/unanswerable| 0/7 | 5/7 | 4/7 | **7/7** | 0/7 |
| 09 path existence       | 1/4 | 2/4 | 1/4 | 2/4 | 3/4 |

## Findings summary

- **H7 — retrieval necessity: CONFIRMED (the primary finding).** `closed_book` 3/52 answers a
  couple of 0-hop attribute lookups from parametric memory and nothing structural. The
  crossover is starkest at 1-hop factoids: `closed_book` 0/5 → `graph_1hop` **5/5**. Retrieval
  is necessary for everything beyond trivial recall.
- **H3 — multi-hop recall (graph holds, vector decays): CONFIRMED, with a ceiling.** Graph
  retrieval is the only thing that answers relational questions — `graph_1hop` 5/5 on 1-hop
  factoids while `vector` is 0/5 and 0 across all content types (02–07). But *deep* multi-hop
  is hard for every arm: 3+-hop (type 04) is 0/52 everywhere; 2-hop traversal tops out at
  `sparqlgen` 2/7. Neighborhood dumping supplies structure but doesn't crack deep traversal —
  only LLM-written SPARQL (`sparqlgen`) scores on aggregation (8/8) and 2-hop.
- **H2 — relational hallucination (graph refuses where vector invents): CONFIRMED for the
  neighborhood arms.** On unanswerable questions (type 08): `graph_2hop` refuses correctly
  7/7, `vector` 5/7, `closed_book` 0/7 (invents an answer every time). Caveat: `graph_sparqlgen`
  is 0/7 — handed a query result set, its generator over-asserts rather than refusing, so the
  "graph refuses" property belongs to the neighborhood retrievers, not the query-gen arm.
- **H1 — token efficiency: PARTIAL / nuanced.** Mean billed input-token premium over the
  `closed_book` baseline (174 tok): `graph_sparqlgen` **+265**, `vector` +674, `graph_1hop`
  +756, `graph_2hop` **+2331**. The realistic deployed graph system (`sparqlgen`) is the
  *leanest* retrieval arm — it passes a compact result set, not a neighborhood — but
  neighborhood-dump retrieval is the *heaviest* arm. So "graph ≪ vector" holds for query-based
  graph retrieval and is *reversed* for neighborhood expansion.
- **H5 — compute / indexing: measured per backend; build wall-clock pending.** Query latency is
  cheap on both sides (`latency_ms` telemetry). On-disk footprint at full corpus: GraphDB index
  923 MB vs Chroma store 1.6 GB — the vector index is the *larger* artifact, against the
  "graphs are heavy" intuition. Build wall-clock not yet captured. Methodology + recorded
  footprint: `analysis/build-cost.md`. Verdict: measure, don't assume.
- **H6 — citability: by construction.** Graph retrievers carry claim-level provenance
  (entity/edge URIs in `sources`); `vector` carries chunk ids. Citation granularity is settled
  by architecture; the scorable version (attribution faithfulness — does each answer claim
  trace to a returned source) is deferred.
- **H4 — fuzzy/semantic recall: DEFERRED to `v1.1.0`.** The type-10 set (6 questions) is
  textbook-famous, so `closed_book` answers from memory and the retrieval comparison isn't
  isolated; the LLM judge scoring them is also uncalibrated (no Cohen's κ yet). **Do not cite
  type-10 accuracy as calibrated.**

Full numbers, per-row telemetry, and the analysis loader: `analysis/explore.ipynb` and
`analysis/FINDINGS.md` at this tag.

## Reproducing this experiment

`produce/questions.jsonl` ships frozen — reproduction stands up the stores and runs the eval,
it does not regenerate the question set. This repo runs through `uv` (no `python`/`pip` on
PATH); the `--extra` must match the retriever.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/joseph-higaki/biomedical-rag-bench
cd biomedical-rag-bench
git checkout v1.0.0

# Prerequisites: secrets/.env (LLM provider key) + secrets/graphdb.license — see secrets/README.md
docker compose up -d                       # GraphDB (license injected as a Compose secret)
make ingest                                # data/rdf/hetionet.ttl + the Chroma collection
# then create the `hetionet` repo and stream-load the Turtle — see ingest/rdf/README.md

# Run one condition (repeat per retriever; --extra must match):
uv run --extra graph --extra generate python eval/run_eval.py --run \
    --retriever graph_neighborhood_1hop --generator anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5 --limit 58
# Or the full deterministic sweep across all five conditions:
make eval-full GEN=anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5
```

Expected runtime: ~2 hours end-to-end on a laptop, dominated by the PubMed fetch (NCBI
rate-limited) and embedding; the GraphDB load is ~5–10 min. The eval sweep itself is minutes.

## Configuration used for the published results

- **Generator (fixed):** `anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5` → resolved `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, temperature 0
- **Judge:** `deterministic-v1` — 9 deterministic question types; the type-10 semantic LLM judge is excluded (H4 deferred)
- **Embeddings:** `sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2` (180-token chunks, 30 overlap)
- **GraphDB:** v11.3.2, `empty` ruleset (no reasoning — the Project 1 baseline; reasoning is Project 2)
- **Corpus:** `full-2c102cb0` — 11,275,571 triples (47,031 nodes / 2,250,197 edges); 77,424 abstracts / 152,943 chunks
- **Hetionet source:** github.com/hetio/hetionet (snapshot pinned by `corpus_build_id`; see `ingest/corpus/`)
- **Question set:** `produce/questions.jsonl` — 52 deterministic questions of 58 total (type-10 excluded)
